overview.short: Malanggad is a hill fort located in the Matheran Hill Range, Thane District, Maharashtra, India.

topPlaces.name: Poshir
topPlaces.description: Poshir is a village near the city of Neral, in the state of Maharashtra, India.

topPlaces.name: Kalyan Airstrip
topPlaces.description: Kalyan Airstrip was located at Nevali village, 6 km (3.7 mi) south of Kalyan railway station and 55 km (34 mi) north of Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport. It was the primary landing ground for the nearby city of Bombay (now Mumbai) as early as 1920, much before the airfields of Juhu and RAF Santa Cruz were established.[2] As a Royal Air Force station during World War II in British India, the airfield served as RAF Kalyan from 1942 to 1947 and was abandoned after the war.

topPlaces.name: Shiv Mandir, Ambarnath
topPlaces.description: The Shiv Mandir of Ambarnath is a historic 11th-century Hindu temple, at Ambarnath near Mumbai, in Maharashtra, India. It is also known as the Ambreshwar Shiva Temple, and known locally as Puratana Shivalaya.

topPlaces.name: Badlapur
topPlaces.description: Badlapur (Marathi pronunciation: [bəd̪lapuːɾ]) is a city in the Thane district, Maharashtra state, India, and is a part of Mumbai Metropolitan Region. Badlapur is a city on the banks of Ulhas River in the Thane district of Maharashtra state in Konkan division.

items.note: Poshir is a village near the city of Neral, in the state of Maharashtra, India.

Kalyan Airstrip was located at Nevali village, 6 km (3.7 mi) south of Kalyan railway station and 55 km (34 mi) north of Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport. It was the primary landing ground for the nearby city of Bombay (now Mumbai) as early as 1920, much before the airfields of Juhu and RAF Santa Cruz were established.[2] As a Royal Air Force station during World War II in British India, the airfield served as RAF Kalyan from 1942 to 1947 and was abandoned after the war.

The Shiv Mandir of Ambarnath is a historic 11th-century Hindu temple, at Ambarnath near Mumbai, in Maharashtra, India. It is also known as the Ambreshwar Shiva Temple, and known locally as Puratana Shivalaya.

Badlapur (Marathi pronunciation: [bəd̪lapuːɾ]) is a city in the Thane district, Maharashtra state, India, and is a part of Mumbai Metropolitan Region. Badlapur is a city on the banks of Ulhas River in the Thane district of Maharashtra state in Konkan division.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Malanggad?
faq.a: The best time to visit Malanggad is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

The nearest airport is Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport, Mumbai (33 km) and the nearest railway station is Kalyan Junction (Mumbai) (15 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Malanggad?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Malanggad at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Malanggad?
faq.a: Stays in Malanggad start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Malanggad?
faq.a: The top attractions are Poshir, Kalyan Airstrip, Shiv Mandir, Ambarnath, Badlapur.
